Indicator evaluation method:  
The Iberai -Biscay-Irish (IBI) Sea Surface Temperature (SST) Ocean Monitoring Indicators 
(OMIs) are derived from the CMEMS Reprocessed European North West Shelf Iberai -Biscay-
Irish Seas (REP ATL) SST L4 product 1. The REP ATL L4 product provides the SSTs that are 
used to compute all the IBI SST OMIs, i.e. the time series of SST anomalies 
(ibi_omi_tempsal_sst_area_averaged_anomalies) and the 2D map of SST trend 
(ibi_omi_tempsal_sst_trend). Anomalies are computed against the 1993 -2014 reference period. 
These indicators belong to the ocean temperature and salinity (TEMPSAL) OMI family. 
                                                           
1 
 ? cmems_SST_ATL_SST_L4_REP_OBSERVATIONS_010_026 
QUID for CMEMS OMIs:  
 IBI_OMI_TEMPSAL_sst_area_averaged_anomalies 
IBI_OMI_TEMPSAL_sst_trend 
  Ref:

10/09/2021 
1.1 
 
 
    Page 3/ 5 
In detail, daily maps of REP ATL L4 data from 1993 were averaged to obtain SST monthly 
maps, which were then used to build the time series of monthly mean SST values averaged 
over the entire basin.  
Trend analysis has been performed by using the X -11 seasonal adjustment pro cedure (Pezzulli 
et al. 2005). This procedure decomposes the input SST time series into the sum of three distinct 
components:  
SSTt = Tt + St + It, 
where Tt is the trend component, St and It the seasonal and the irregular components. The X -11 
procedure has the effect of filtering the input time series with Tt acting as a low bandpass filter 
for interannual variations. The only difference with respect to the standard X -11 procedure is 
that we substituted the 13 -term moving-average window (used to estimate Tt ) with a 25 -term 
moving-average window, i.e. from 1-year to 2-year low-pass filter. This makes more robust the 
estimation of the trend component Tt. 
Mann-Kendall test (Mann, 1945; Kendall, 1975) was applied to Tt in order to assess whether 
there is a monotonic upward or downward trend over time and Sens’s method (Sen, 1968) was 
subsequently applied to estimate the slope (°C/year) of the trend component Tt and its 95% 
confidence interval.  
 
 
Dataset name: ibi_omi_tempsal_sst_area_averaged_anomalies 
This OMI product includes SST anomaly, given as monthly mean time series starting on 1993 
and averaged over the Iberai-Biscay-Irish Seas. 
 
Dataset name: ibi_omi_tempsal_sst_trend 
This OMI product includes the  trend map for the Iberai -Biscay-Irish Seas , i.e. the r ate of 
change of SST (°C/year). Trend computation is based on X-11 seasonal adjustment procedure. 
 
Scientific validation: 
The reliability of the IBI SST OMI products is strictly related to the quality and accuracy of the 
REP ATL L4 SST product, which is va lidated in the CMEMS REP ATL QUID (see 
http://marine.copernicus.eu/documents/QUID/CMEMS-SST-QUID-010-026.pdf). The 
accuracy of the REP ATL L4 product has been assessed  by comparison to independent in situ 
data for the period 2005 -2016. Resul ts show an overall mean bias and root mean square 
difference (RMSD) of the differences between REP ATL and in situ data of -0.07 and 0.46 K, 
respectively. The time series of monthly averaged mean bias and RMSD is shown in Figure 1. 
The overall validation i ndicates that this product represents a valuable SST dataset for climate 
studies. 
We recall that the REP ATL L4 product is a collection of 38 years of daily (nighttime)  
interpolated (level -4, L4) satellite -based estimates of the foundation SST over the Eu ropean
